content.full_text:
March 23, 1976

The Honorable Robert E. Badham  
Member, California Assembly  
State Capitol  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Assemblyman Badham:

As you are aware, a formal complaint, C-36/76, was filed with the Fair Political Practices Commission on March 10, 1976, alleging that you violated Government Code Section 89001. A copy of that complaint was hand-delivered to your office on Thursday, March 18, 1976. The complaint will be considered by the Fair Political Practices Commission in Executive Session at its April 6 - 8, 1976, meeting.

The staff will recommend to the Commission at that time that no action be taken. The complaint is based on an assumption that a federal declaration of candidacy triggers Section 89001 of the Government Code, but the staff does not agree with that interpretation of Section 89001. Rather, the staff believes that only a declaration of candidacy, filed pursuant to Elections Code Section 6490 is the declaration that triggers the provisions of Section 89001. It is the staff's belief, however, that if the Commission interprets Section 89001 differently, such an interpretation of the Section would be applied prospectively.

You will be advised of the Commission's decision after its April meeting.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern  
Acting Director  
Enforcement Division
